commit.message:
Fix restart sync skipping when empty rounds cause equal nullifier counts

The EpochStateResponse handler only checked nullifier_count to decide
whether a restarted node needs to sync. When vertices finalized during
downtime contain no transactions (empty consensus rounds), nullifier
counts match and the node incorrectly marks itself as Synced.

Now also compares commitment_root, which differs whenever any vertices
have been finalized (even empty ones), ensuring sync always triggers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add run-wrfchem-rotate-plume.py for WRF-Chem 3D cloud fraction

New standalone script reads CLDFRA from wrfout files, computes column
cloud fraction as 1 - prod(1 - CLDFRA, axis=0), and saves in the
standard rotavg format. Subdirectory lookup uses *mmdd glob to handle
folders named '0915' or '0823-to-20250915'. Wind direction is taken
from the paired no2 file.

feat: add lifetime kWh polling from TWC slaves

Sends FBEB power status request every 60 seconds to each slave.
Parses FDEB response for lifetime kWh (4-byte uint32) and per-phase
voltages. Publishes lifetime_kwh in MQTT status messages.

commit.message:
Add eunit tests for 0%-cover ops/dispatch modules (BT-1088) (#1145)

## Summary

- Add eunit tests for 5 previously uncovered runtime modules:
`beamtalk_datetime`, `beamtalk_regex`, `beamtalk_array_ops`,
`beamtalk_collection_ops`, `beamtalk_behaviour_intrinsics`
- Fix unused variable warning `_Selector` in `beamtalk_datetime.erl:306`

## Key changes

- **beamtalk_datetime_tests.erl** (new): 40+ tests covering
constructors, accessors, conversion, arithmetic, comparison,
`has_method/1`, and error paths
- **beamtalk_regex_tests.erl** (new): 40+ tests covering `from:`,
`from:options:`, string helpers (`matches_regex`, `first_match`,
`all_matches`, `replace_regex`, `split_regex`), and error paths
- **beamtalk_array_ops_tests.erl** (new): 30+ tests covering
`from_list`, `size`, `at`, `at_put`, `do`, `includes`, `collect`,
`select`, `inject_into`, `print_string`, and error paths
- **beamtalk_collection_ops_tests.erl** (new): 8 tests for
`inject_into/3` and `to_list/1`
- **beamtalk_behaviour_intrinsics_tests.erl** (new): 15+ tests for
`metaclassNew`, `classClass`, `className`, `classLocalMethods`,
`classFieldNames`, `classIncludesSelector`, `classDoc`, `classSetDoc`,
`classSetMethodDoc` using minimal setup + class_builder

Linear:
https://linear.app/beamtalk/issue/BT-1088/add-eunit-tests-for-0percent-cover-opsdispatch-modules

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Tests**
* Added comprehensive test suites for arrays, collections, datetime,
regex, and behavior introspection to improve reliability and cover many
edge cases and error paths.

* **Refactor**
* Minor internal cleanup in datetime validation to silence
unused-variable warnings and tidy implementation details.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

feat(web): add HTML ids to all UI elements for testing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

BuilderState simplification: remove static metadata duplication (BT-1078) (#1146)

## Summary

ADR 0050 Phase 5: Makes `__beamtalk_meta/0` the single source of truth
for static class metadata by removing redundant keys from
`BuilderState`.

- **Codegen**: Extract `build_meta_map_doc` shared helper used by both
`__beamtalk_meta/0` and `BuilderState.meta`. Remove `fieldSpecs`,
`methodSpecs`, `modifiers`, `classMethods`, `methodReturnTypes`,
`classMethodReturnTypes` from BuilderState (~300 lines removed)
- **Runtime**: `init/1` and `apply_class_info/2` prefer `ClassInfo.meta`
over `read_meta(Module)` (fixes `erlang:function_exported/3` returning
`false` during `on_load`). New `meta_to_methods/2` converts meta
method_info to internal format. Backward compat via `maps:merge` for
explicit return types
- **Tests**: Update inline codegen tests and 20 snapshot files for new
BuilderState structure
- Remove deprecated tuple-list `methods`/`class_methods` keys from
`__beamtalk_meta/0`

## Linear Issue

https://linear.app/beamtalk/issue/BT-1078

## Test plan

- [x] `just test` — 2498 Rust tests pass
- [x] `just test-stdlib` — 733 BUnit tests pass (12 pre-existing
failures)
- [x] `cd runtime && rebar3 eunit` — 2413 Erlang tests pass
- [x] `just ci` — full CI clean

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Refactor**
* Consolidated class/method/field metadata into a single compile-time
meta payload propagated into registration and BuilderState.
* **New Features**
* Per-method sealed status included in emitted metadata and available at
runtime.
* Generated modules now embed the unified meta payload for on-load
access.
* **Bug Fixes**
* Improved on-load and hot-patch handling so static meta (methods,
fields, seals, return types) is consistently honored.
* **Tests**
* Updated expectations to validate metadata lives under the unified meta
structure.
* **Documentation**
  * Updated ADR/phase notes to reflect the metadata-centric behavior.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

Implement a complete MCP server for querying Tableau data sources via the VizQL Data Service API.

- Three MCP tools: list_datasources, get_datasource_metadata, query_datasource
- Robust error handling and token expiration management
- Support for complex queries with filters and sorts

Files added:
- index.js: MCP server with three tools
- tableau.js: Tableau API client with authentication
- package.json: Project dependencies and configuration
- .env.example: Template for environment variables
- README.md: Comprehensive setup and usage documentation

chore: remove tracked files that should be gitignored

Remove _bmad/, .claude/, .github/, and _bmad-output/ from tracking.
These directories were accidentally committed despite being in .gitignore.
Files remain on disk but are no longer tracked by git.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: reduce WFO total_timesteps 30M → 10M per segment

Convergence analysis on run 6576 shows critic_loss converges by 3M steps
and actor_loss stabilizes by 5M. Cosine entropy schedule was ineffective
at 30M (only 12% of schedule completed by 10M). At 10M the full
-0.7 → -1.0 annealing completes within the effective training window.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add optimize command and periodic compaction during scan

LanceDB appends each write as a new fragment file, causing full-table
scans to open one file per photo as the index grows. Fix by:

- New `faces optimize` command: compacts all tables via table.optimize()
  and creates a BTREE scalar index on md5 for O(log n) lookups.
- scan: compact photos and faces tables every 1000 new photos (and once
  at the end), preventing fragmentation from accumulating mid-run.

Fix sudo password delivery and logging for permissions/cronjobs scripts

sudo -S password delivery, fixing hangs caused by shell escaping issues
with special characters in passwords. Remove unnecessary sudo from
chmod +x. Change key log messages from info to warning level so
permissions and cronjobs progress is visible when verbose=false.

commit.message:
Fix bounding box tiles saving to wrong folder in existing projects

When running a new bounding box acquisition inside an existing project,
tiles were created in a sibling directory (named after the user-entered
sample name) instead of inside the actual project folder. Fixed by
deriving the sample name from the project path rather than the dialog
input when an existing project is open.

Fix sonar animation on mobile — animate r directly instead of transform scale

transform-box: fill-box is unreliable on mobile Safari, causing rings
to scale from the SVG origin instead of the circle center. Animating
the r attribute via CSS keyframes works correctly everywhere.

commit.message:
Add Google Flights URL paste-to-autofill on Book page

Parse the protobuf-encoded tfs parameter from Google Flights URLs to
extract airport codes, dates, airline codes, and flight numbers.
Slices are preserved as Google encodes them (outbound + return).
Times show as missing since Google doesn't encode exact times in URLs.

health checks to timeout. These resources are applied correctly
regardless — waiting just blocks reconciliation status.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix RSpec/ScatteredLet: skip shared groups (43 FP fix)

RuboCop's example_group_with_body? only matches ExampleGroups
(describe/context/feature), not SharedGroups (shared_examples,
shared_examples_for, shared_context). Our cop was incorrectly
running inside shared groups because is_rspec_example_group()
includes shared group names. Added an early return for shared
group method names to match RuboCop's behavior.

that caused "Insufficient payment" errors despite correct transfers.

test(abi): add iconv, isoc, pthread, setjmp, and startup ABI test suites

Expand ABI conformance coverage with five new test files and pwd test
improvements:

- iconv_abi_test.rs: validate iconv_open/iconv/iconv_close contracts
  for charset conversion between UTF-8, ISO-8859-1, etc.
- isoc_abi_test.rs: C standard library function ABI tests (string,
  memory, math, stdlib, errno, time, stdio)
- pthread_abi_test.rs: POSIX threading ABI tests (mutex, rwlock, cond,
  thread create/join, TLS)
- setjmp_abi_test.rs: setjmp/longjmp ABI contract tests
- startup_abi_test.rs: program startup/exit ABI tests (__libc_start_main,
  atexit, environ, auxval)
- pwd_abi_test.rs: fix existing test assertions
- Update beads issue tracker with current status

commit.message:
Add MES v2 BE_TIME exit (75 bars) and dashboard improvements

Close stale MES v2 trades after 75 bars (~1h15m). Re-swept from
original N=275 with finer granularity: N=75 has best OOS Sharpe
(1.502 vs 0.808), PF (1.292), and MaxDD (-$673 vs -$999).

Live engine: bar counter in strategy.py, off-by-one fixed to match
backtest. BE_TIME is bar-close only (not intra-bar), fires after
TP/trail checks, correctly cancels OCO brackets on exit.

Dashboard: MES card shows "45 / 75 bars" countdown, trade log shows
BE_TIME as "Stale Exit" (orange), TP1 as "Partial TP" (cyan).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
v2.6.0: egress gate, Phase D integration lane, v3 bake-in placeholders

Egress gate (GOV_GAP_EGRESS_001): outbound data-flow policy gate with
payload/destination classification, 6 rules in explicit precedence,
receipt redaction. Completes v2.x threat hardening track (provenance →
chain → egress). 66 tests.

Phase D integration lane: governor signals preflight CLI (local JSONL,
no daemon dependency), signals.preflight daemon RPC, load_latest_envelopes
standalone loader, Phase D wired into sim pipeline (6 signal kinds).

v3 bake-in placeholders: principal_ref on GateReceipt (sha256:<hex>
format, null in v2), session block in governor.hello (principal,
auth_method, session_token — all null/local in v2), signals_preflight
capability flag, [security] daemon config section documented.

Scar fingerprints, doc renames (SCREAMING_SNAKE consistency),
REFERENCES.md (design ancestors + field evidence), AGENTS.md refresh.

~14,560 tests, all green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: extract salary from custom CMS and micro1 job pages

Three related fixes for surfacing compensation data:

1. extractCompensationFromHtml() helper (new)
   Scans a job-description HTML page for a "Compensation" heading
   (any h1–h6, optionally wrapped in inline tags like <strong>) and
   extracts a salary range from the following paragraph. Handles:
     • dash-separated ranges:  "100k-200k per year" → $100K – $200K
     • dollar full-number:     "$80,000 - $120,000" → $80K – $120K
     • dollar-K notation:      "$90K – $130K"       → $90K – $130K
     • prose "and" separator:  "between $80k and $120k" → $80K – $120K
     • hourly:                 "$45 per hour"       → $45/hr

2. fetchCustomJobs: per-job page fetching (new)
   After collecting job links from the listing page, fetches each
   individual job page in parallel (Promise.allSettled) and populates
   job.compensation via extractCompensationFromHtml. Failures are
   silently ignored. Covers sites like north.cloud that use a generic
   CMS with a freeform Compensation section.

3. fetchMicro1Jobs: use ideal_yearly_compensation as salary (fix)
   For micro1 core team jobs, ideal_yearly_compensation is not just a
   type-filter signal — it IS the salary string (e.g. "$350K - $600K/yr").
   Now strips the /yr suffix and passes it through formatSalary so it
   renders as "$350K – $600K" on the job card.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
